mechanism_narrative: A persistently elevated federal funds rate relative to its trailing 12-month average signals tight monetary conditions that raise the discount rate on equity cash flows, producing a persistent drag on S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon. The effect is continuous and does not require a discrete trigger.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 60-day forward returns stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or high-rate periods show statistically indistinguishable returns from low-rate periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises above a 0.3 percentage point increase over the trailing 3 months, it signals labor market deterioration that historically precedes risk-off equity repricing, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.3pp over trailing 3 months
failure_condition: In more than 50% of historical instances where the trigger fires, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive, or Rank IC conditional on trigger is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous weakness in both retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signals a broad-based consumer demand contraction that is not captured by either series alone, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week as markets reprice growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month decline AND HOUST month-over-month decline simultaneously
failure_condition: The joint-decline signal produces 5-day forward returns statistically indistinguishable from the unconditional baseline (p > 0.05), or the interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC versus either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery regime following the 2008-2009 recession, sustained month-over-month increases in industrial production (INDPRO) signal expanding manufacturing capacity utilization that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter. This mechanism is expected to be weaker or absent outside recovery phases.
evidence_refs: fact:economy in post-2008-2009 recession recovery phase as of 2011
failure_condition: During confirmed recovery-regime windows, 60-day forward returns following INDPRO uptrends fail to exceed unconditional 60-day return distribution at the 10% significance level.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects strengthening labor income and consumer spending capacity, which supports forward corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the following month. The relationship is continuous and always active.
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing 3-month PAYEMS growth and 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0% while CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation simultaneously accelerates, markets interpret this as a signal of impending aggressive monetary tightening, causing a short-horizon negative repricing of equities within one trading week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.0% AND CPIAUCSL YoY inflation rising
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following joint trigger events are not significantly negative relative to baseline (p > 0.05) across a 3-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, deceleration in real GDP growth (GDPC1) below its trailing 4-quarter average signals fading aggregate demand momentum that precedes equity market weakness, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter. In early-cycle recovery regimes, the same deceleration is less predictive due to base-effect noise.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth falls below trailing 4-quarter average
failure_condition: When conditioned on late-cycle regime dating, 60-day forward returns following GDPC1 deceleration are not significantly negative (p > 0.05) versus unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A joint pattern of rising UNRATE combined with stagnant or declining PAYEMS growth signals a genuine labor market inflection that neither series confirms alone, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month as earnings expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises month-over-month AND PAYEMS month-over-month change is negative or flat
failure_condition: The joint UNRATE-PAYEMS trigger produces 20-day forward return Rank IC below 0.03, or fails to outperform either single-series signal in out-of-sample testing.
